missions.title: Create And Integrate QA Agent
missions.description: Lord of the Machines currently does not have a QA agent role. Implement and integrate a QA agent into the mission workflow, including role prompt, runtime phase support, runner wiring, and baseline tests.

metadata.goal: Add a dedicated QA capability to improve delivery reliability and verification quality.

decision_defaults.qa_phase_skip_policy: QA phase is mandatory by default. It can be skipped only for emergency hotfixes or missions with no code changes, and the skip reason must be explicitly documented.
qa_agent_mvp_responsibilities: Run required diagnostics/test profiles for changed code.
Validate mission acceptance criteria and required artifacts.
Verify no critical unresolved issues remain before completion.
Publish a QA verification summary with pass/fail evidence.

workflow_constraints: Implement real code changes; do not stop at planning.
Keep changes scoped to mission/runtime/prompting/runner wiring and tests.
Do not remove existing role behavior unless replacing it intentionally with QA integration.
Run local tests for touched mission modules.
Decision defaults in metadata are already approved and must be applied directly; do not open extra follow-up loops for those points.

deliverables.description: QA role prompt and role profile are added.
Mission runtime phase mapping and transitions include QA phase integration.
Mission runner creates and wires a QA role executor.
Tests updated or added to validate QA integration.
Document what was implemented, where, and how QA fits in the workflow.

acceptance_criteria: A QA agent role exists in prompts and can be created by the role factory.
Mission runtime flow includes a QA phase and role mapping.
Mission runner wiring includes QA agent executor.

missions.title: Add OS System Command Capability To Developer Tool
missions.description: Implement a system-level command method in software_development_environment so developer agents can execute operating-system commands with disk/system visibility under explicit policy control.

metadata.goal: Enable controlled OS-level command execution for autonomous development tasks.

decision_defaults.system_command_policy: OS-level command execution must be explicit and policy-driven. It should be disabled by default for read-only roles and enabled only where explicitly configured.
decision_defaults.backward_compatibility_policy: Existing workspace-scoped run_command behavior must keep working as-is.

workflow_constraints: Implement real code changes; do not stop at planning.
Do not remove or silently weaken existing safety controls.
Keep workspace-scoped command execution available for current workflows.
Add tests for new command method behavior and policy gating.
Document the new capability, limits, and risk controls.

deliverables.description: Add OS-level command execution method with explicit policy checks.
Expose new method in tool handlers/definition according to permission policy.
Document schema for the OS-level command method.
Add explicit permission flags for OS-level command execution.
Add/update tests for method exposure, execution policy gating, and command result behavior.
Explain how to use the OS-level command capability, required policy flags, and safety notes.

acceptance_criteria: A new OS-level command method exists in software_development_environment.
OS-level command execution is policy-gated and explicitly configurable.
Current workspace-scoped command behavior remains available.
Automated tests cover the new method and policy behavior.
Documentation explains usage and safety controls.
